notes: Foundational IAQ for restoration/cleaning. Depict the core taught tools and methods: air quality monitoring instruments, containment with negative air, moisture/particle metering. Australian restoration settings (AS 1668.2 context), metric, professional PPE. No on-image text, no brand names/logos.

images.context: Course hero for 'Introduction to Air Quality Fundamentals'
images.prompt: A cleaning and restoration professional in gloves and an N95 respirator assessing indoor air quality in a bright Australian home, mid-distance and over the shoulder, holding a handheld air quality monitor while a portable HEPA air scrubber runs nearby. Fresh, clean interior, natural daylight, calm and professional, focus on the assessment task. Realistic professional photography, aspirational.
images.negativePrompt: no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing imagery, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no readable screen numbers, no visible mould or filth, no clutter

images.context: Module 5 — strategies: containment with plastic sheeting and a negative air machine with HEPA
images.prompt: A neatly built containment barrier of clear plastic sheeting with a zipper access in a bright Australian interior, a negative air machine with a HEPA filter drawing air from the work zone and ducting venting away, a technician in PPE checking the setup in the mid-distance. Orderly, safe airflow control, professional. Realistic professional photography.

images.context: Module 4 — assessment tools: moisture meter and particle counter in use
images.prompt: A close hands-on-task view of a technician's gloved hands pressing a pin moisture meter against a skirting board while a handheld particle counter sits ready on a nearby surface, checking conditions in a bright Australian interior. Focus on the instruments and the hands, shallow depth of field, precise and professional. Realistic professional photography.
